{{Short description="SI base unit of electric current}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Ampere
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Electric current]], [[International System of Units]], [[Elementary charge]]
| unit_system = [[SI base unit]]
| unit_of = [[Electric current]]
| symbol = A
| named_after = [[André-Marie Ampère]]
}}

The '''ampere''' (symbol: '''A'''), often shortened to '''amp''', is the [[SI base unit]] of [[electric current]]. It is named after [[André-Marie Ampère]], one of the founders of [[electromagnetism]].

Since 2019, the ampere is defined by fixing the numerical value of the [[elementary charge]] <math>e</math> to 1.602176634×10⁻¹⁹ when expressed in the unit [[coulomb]] (C = A·s):

:<math>1\ \text{A} = \frac{e}{1.602176634 \times 10^{-19}}\ \text{s}^{-1}</math>

Equivalently, one ampere is the current corresponding to the flow of <math>1/(1.602176634\times 10^{-19}) \approx 6.24\times 10^{18}</math> elementary charges per [[second]].

== Historical definition ==
Before 2019, the ampere was defined via the magnetic force between two parallel conductors: one ampere was the constant current that, if maintained in two straight parallel conductors of infinite length and negligible cross-section placed 1 metre apart in vacuum, would produce a force of 2×10⁻⁷ N/m between them. This definition fixed <math>\mu_0 = 4\pi\times 10^{-7}\ \text{N/A}^2</math>.

== Multiples ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Submultiples !! Multiples
|-
| milliampere (mA) = 10⁻³ A || kiloampere (kA) = 10³ A
|-
| microampere (μA) = 10⁻⁶ A || megaampere (MA) = 10⁶ A
|}

== Ampere in Ohm's law ==
{{Main|Ohm's law}}
:<math>I = \frac{V}{R}</math>
One ampere flows through a 1 [[ohm]] resistor when 1 [[volt]] is applied.

== Power relation ==
:<math>P = IV</math>
One ampere at one volt delivers one [[watt]] of power.

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Coulomb]]
* [[Electric current]]
* [[International System of Units]]
* [[Ohm's law]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:SI base units]]
[[Category:Units of electric current]]
[[Category:Electromagnetism]]
